Header file for managing accelerometer data and memory logging in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
This C header file declares several static functions and includes necessary headers for its implementation. The file includes headers such as `accel_service.h`, `constants_worker.h`, `helper_worker.h`, `fourier.h`, and `raw_stats.h`, which suggests dependencies on external modules for functionality related to acceleration data processing, constants, helper functions, Fourier transformations, and raw statistics. The static functions declared include [`write_blk_buf_to_persist`](<#write_blk_buf_to_persist>), [`summ_datalog`](<#summ_datalog>), [`reset_summ_metrics`](<#reset_summ_metrics>), [`accel_data_handler`](<#accel_data_handler>), [`init_mem_log`](<#init_mem_log>), [`init`](<#init>), and [`deinit`](<#deinit>), which are likely used for data handling, logging, and initialization tasks. The [`main`](<#main>) function is also declared, indicating that this file is part of an executable program. The use of `#pragma once` ensures that the file is included only once in a single compilation, preventing duplicate definitions.

### write\_blk\_buf\_to\_persist<!-- {{#callable_declaration:write_blk_buf_to_persist}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/kraepelin_reference/ProjectK_worker.h#L11>)

Writes buffered data to persistent storage and manages data transmission.
- **Description**: Use this function to store buffered data into persistent storage and manage data transmission based on Bluetooth connectivity. It increments the storage index and writes data if there is available space. If a Bluetooth connection is present, it attempts to send data to a phone or server. If storage is nearly full and no connection is available, it prompts the user to connect their phone. This function must be called when data needs to be stored or transmitted.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`write_blk_buf_to_persist`](<ProjectK_worker.c.md#write_blk_buf_to_persist>)  (Implementation)


---
### summ\_datalog<!-- {{#callable_declaration:summ_datalog}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/kraepelin_reference/ProjectK_worker.h#L13>)

Logs summary data and updates persistent storage.
- **Description**: Use this function to log actigraphy data, update summary metrics, and manage persistent storage. It processes data in blocks, updating a buffer with orientation and activity metrics. It also calculates and stores calorie and step count data. This function must be called periodically to ensure data is logged and stored correctly. It handles the transition of data blocks and resets counters when necessary.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`summ_datalog`](<ProjectK_worker.c.md#summ_datalog>)  (Implementation)

### reset\_summ\_metrics<!-- {{#callable_declaration:reset_summ_metrics}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/kraepelin_reference/ProjectK_worker.h#L15>)

Resets all summary metrics to zero.
- **Description**: Use this function to reset all summary metrics to their initial state of zero. This is typically necessary when you need to clear previous data and start fresh calculations. Ensure that any necessary data processing is complete before calling this function, as it will erase all current metric values.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`reset_summ_metrics`](<ProjectK_worker.c.md#reset_summ_metrics>)  (Implementation)

### init\_mem\_log<!-- {{#callable_declaration:init_mem_log}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../tests/fw/services/activity/kraepelin_reference/ProjectK_worker.h#L19>)

Initializes memory for logging data.
- **Description**: Use this function to allocate and initialize memory buffers needed for data logging. It sets up storage arrays for different axes and a work array, and allocates a block buffer for data summarization. This function must be called before any data logging operations to ensure that all necessary memory is available. It also initializes a filter and sets a flag indicating the filter is not yet primed. This function is typically called during the initialization phase of an application.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`init_mem_log`](<ProjectK_worker.c.md#init_mem_log>)  (Implementation)
